hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The value 'false' indicates the HTML content is a single string, while 'true' indicates it should be returned as an array of string sections.

trigger_keywords: test cases for feature1 slide rendering
show me feature1 slide rendering test cases
what are the feature1 slide rendering inputs and outputs
give me the feature1_slide_rendering test file

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_theme_catalog.json.description: Manage a catalog of named themes, including existence checks, deletion, fallback selection, imported metadata resolution, imported size-property resolution, and circular-import errors.

cases.expected_output:
size=2
themes=["test1","test2"]
check_0=true
check_1=true
check_2=false
check_3=true
final_size=1

feature11_theme_packaging.json.description: Package registered theme CSS into slide-ready CSS, rolling up imports, adding caller-supplied CSS, scoping printable rules, and ignoring invalid caller-supplied CSS snippets.

feature1_slide_rendering.json.description: Render Markdown slide content into section-based HTML, preserving slide boundaries and optionally returning one HTML item per slide.

cases.expected_output:
html_is_array=false html_item_count=1 section_count=1 html=
comments=[[]]

feature2_comments.json.description: Extract presentational HTML comments as slide comments while stripping them from rendered slide HTML, without treating comments inside code as presenter comments.

input.markdown:
# foo
<!-----comment!-->

<!--
supports
multiline
-->
## bar
comment<!-- inline -->Test

### <!-- comment in header -->

cases.expected_output:
html_is_array=false html_item_count=1 section_count=1 html=

foo

bar

commentTest

comments=[["comment!","supports\nmultiline","inline"]]

feature3_directives.json.description: Parse slide directives from front matter and comments so global directives apply to the deck and local directives apply to appropriate slides.

cases.expected_output:
html_is_array=false html_item_count=1 section_count=2 html=
comments=[[],[]]

feature4_heading_divider.json.description: Split slides automatically before configured heading levels, while preserving normal slide rendering when the divider option is disabled or invalid.

cases.expected_output:
html_is_array=false html_item_count=1 section_count=1 html=

1st

2nd

3rd

4th

comments=[[]]

feature5_header_footer.json.description: Render header and footer directives into repeated slide header/footer elements, including inline Markdown inside those elements and ignoring invalid non-text values.

cases.expected_output:
html_is_array=false html_item_count=1 section_count=2 html=
text

Page 1

text

Page 2

comments=[[],[]]

feature6_images_and_backgrounds.json.description: Interpret image option text as dimensions, text-color shorthand, and background-image/background-color/background-size directives while suppressing background images from normal image output.

cases.expected_output:
html_is_array=false html_item_count=1 section_count=1 html=

comments=[[]]

feature7_inline_svg_backgrounds.json.description: When inline SVG rendering is enabled, wrap slide content in SVG/foreignObject markup and render advanced backgrounds as separate background figures.

feature8_inline_styles.json.description: Remove embedded style elements from slide HTML, append their CSS to rendered stylesheet output, support scoped inline styles, and suppress valid theme imports.

feature9_theme_parsing.json.description: Parse theme stylesheets into named themes with metadata, imports, slide size properties, pixel conversions for absolute units, and normalized failures for missing required metadata.

cases.expected_output:
name=test-theme
css=/* @theme test-theme */
metadata={"theme":"test-theme"}
imports=[]
width=undefined
height=undefined
width_px=undefined
height_px=undefined

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains markdown and optionally options object with htmlArray flag
_constraints.output_rule: Return object containing html_is_array (boolean), html_item_count (int), section_count (int), html (string or array), and comments (array)

hide.replacement: a specific default state value representing no HTML array
hide.oracle_response: The value 'false' indicates the HTML content is a single string, while 'true' indicates it should be returned as an array of string sections.
